content.problemStatement:
There is an N×MN\times MN×M grid.

The rows are numbered 111 to NNN from top to bottom and columns are numbered 111 to MMM from left to right. Alice is at coordinate (X,Y)(X,Y)(X,Y).

Alice needs to calculate the bitwise XOR of Manhattan distances of all the coordinates in the grid from the point (X,Y)(X,Y)(X,Y).

Note that Manhattan distance between points (X1,Y1)(X_1,Y_1)(X1​,Y1​) and (X2,Y2)(X_2,Y_2)(X2​,Y2​) is calculated as ∣X1−X2∣+∣Y1−Y2∣|X_1 - X_2| + |Y_1 - Y_2|∣X1​−X2​∣+∣Y1​−Y2​∣.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of rows and the number of columns in the grid, respectively.
The next line contains two space-separated integers XXX and YYY - Alice's Coordinates.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the bitwise XOR of Manhattan distances of all the coordinates in the grid from the point (X,Y)(X,Y)(X,Y).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Alice is at coordinate (2,2).

The Manhattan distance between (2,2)(2,2)(2,2) and (2,2)(2,2)(2,2) is 000.
The Manhattan distance between {(1,2),(2,1),(3,2),(2,3)}\{(1,2) , (2,1) , (3,2) , (2,3)\}{(1,2),(2,1),(3,2),(2,3)} and (2,2)(2,2)(2,2) is 111.
The Manhattan distance between {(1,1),(1,3),(3,1),(3,3)}\{(1,1) , (1,3) , (3,1) , (3,3)\}{(1,1),(1,3),(3,1),(3,3)} and (2,2)(2,2)(2,2) is 222.

So, the bitwise XOR of Manhattan distances from point (2,2)(2,2)(2,2) is 000.
